rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO MENDOZA et al VS. CAFE COCOMO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 11, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT CLUB COCOMO, AKA CAFE COCOMO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off calendar. Defendant has failed to provide a courtesy copy of the motion per the court's order of July 29, 2011. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO MENDOZA et al VS. CAFE COCOMO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 11, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT ABITSCH & ABITSCH LLC, JEFFREY ABITSCH, GEORGE ABITSCH MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. The court finds that under Barnes v. Black (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 1473, the fact that the injury occurred off the premises is not dispositive of whether a landlord is liable. Under Barnes the scope of a landlord's duty is determined by Rowland factors. in looking at the record before the court it is clear that there are several triable issues of fact going to the Rowland factors. As such, defendants have not met their burden. Defendant's objection to the Applebaum deposition is denied. Defendants have failed to show their own due diligence in securing deposition testimony of Applebaum prior to his death. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DAWN M THOMPSON VS. BANKERS INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion For New Trial
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 11, 2011, line 4. PLAINTIFF DAWN THOMPSON, ANTHONY JAO, AJ'S HARDWOOD FLOORING, INC Notice Of Hearing On Motion For New Trial. Plaintiff's motion for a new trial is granted. First, plaintiff's motion is not procedurally improper. A motion for a new trial standard does not mirror the reconsideration standard Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 859, fn.29. Plaintiff cites error of law as the basis for this motion and defendant has responded on the merits. Plaintiff cites new cases for the court to consider, Houser v. Gilbert (N.D. 1986) 389 N.W.2d 626, and Estate of Pennington v. Wolfe (D.Kan 2003) 262 F.Supp2d 1254. The Court finds these cases persuasive in supporting plaintiff's position that the failure to remove an obstruction from the roadway is independent from the operation of the vehicle and that the Court should find coverage. Accordingly, the motion for a new trial is granted based on error of law per C.C.P. 657 (7). =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LEONARD JAMES MILLER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO PLANNING et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 11, 2011, line 9. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the 1st, 2nd and 4th causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's claims are barred because they are not based on statute. Gov. Code 815. Defendant's demurrer to the 4th cause of action is also sustained without leave to amend because it is preempted by the exclusive remedy provisionts of the California Workers Compensation Act. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JOANNE REINHART VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Osc Re Preliminary Injunction And Tro.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 11, 2011, line 11. Order to Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiff's request for a preliminary injunction to stop foreclosure proceedings pending trial is granted. Plaintiff must post a bond in the amount of $75,000. The court finds that there is a likelihood of plaintiff prevailing on the merits of her fraud allegation and the balance of harm tips in her favor particularly given her current medical condition. =(302/LMG)
